title: Pond 1.1 — a big update to how it plays and feels
body:
Pond 1.1 is here — the biggest update yet to how the pond plays and feels.

- **Per-emitter rates, and drag to move them.** Each emitter keeps its own drip rate now: tap an emitter to change its rate, drag it to reposition it (even several at once, one per finger), and a few at different rates weave into polyrhythms. Each emitter's marker pulses in time with its own drip, so you can see the rhythm at a glance.
- **Undo and redo, in the drawer.** Painting, Generate, Clear, Transpose, emitters, scales — it all steps back and forward.
- **Transpose the whole pond** up or down, right by the scale picker.
- **Six more scales, fifteen in all** — the full set of modes plus harmonic and melodic minor and the Japanese Hirajoshi.
- **Velocity for wave interference.** Set the range with Base, Min and Max; the overlapping waves decide where each note lands.
- **A movable paint bar.** Drag it by the handle to the top or bottom so it never covers the cells you want.
- **Pond follows your host.** As a plugin in AUM, Logic or Cubasis, it now starts and stops with the host transport, so the emitters sit in time with the rest of your session.

title: Re-deemer is coming to iOS
body: Re-deemer is coming to iPhone and iPad. The cassette tape echo that began as a desktop plug-in is now in development for iOS, with a TestFlight beta on the way.

title: Hexatone's manual is now on the web
body: The full Hexatone manual is now a page on the site, with a downloadable PDF — covering tunings, the built-in sampler, isomorphic layouts, and MPE. The free in-browser **SFZ Builder** turns a folder of samples into an instrument too.

title: Pond is out on the App Store
body: Pond — a ripple sequencer for iPad — is available now. Drop a stone in the water and let the wave play the notes: tap to send ripples, plant emitters, and watch real wave interference shape the dynamics.

title: Anima — a wind synth on the way
body: Work has begun on **Anima**, a physical-modeling wind synthesizer for iPad and the companion to Hexatone. No oscillators — air resonating in a pipe — and microtonal to the core.
